source: Adapted from World Bank Climate and Disaster Risk Screening guidelines for road projects. Ratings are illustrative -- project-specific assessment required. Likelihood and consequence scales should be calibrated to local climate data and road network context.
framework: Climate Risk Screening Matrix for Road Infrastructure

hazards.description: Increased rainfall intensity causing culvert overtopping, embankment washout, road submergence, and erosion

likelihood_criteria.low: Arid/semi-arid area, well-drained terrain, no flood history, rainfall <800mm/yr
likelihood_criteria.medium: Moderate rainfall area, some flood history, flat terrain near water bodies
likelihood_criteria.high: High rainfall area (>1500mm/yr), flood-prone lowland, history of flood damage, climate projections show increasing intensity

consequence_criteria.minor: Low-traffic road (<200 vpd), alternative routes available, minor economic disruption
consequence_criteria.moderate: Medium-traffic road (200-1000 vpd), limited alternatives, moderate economic disruption
consequence_criteria.major: High-traffic road (>1000 vpd) or strategic road, no alternative route, critical for community access

adaptation_measures: Increase culvert capacity by 20-30% (Skill #31, design Q x 1.2-1.3)
Raise road embankment above projected flood levels
Improve drainage channel capacity (Skill #30)
Specify erosion protection at outlets and slopes (Skill #32)
Consider increasing design return period (e.g., 25-year to 50-year)
Provide scour protection at bridge foundations (Skill #39)

hazards.description: High temperatures causing pavement softening, rutting, binder degradation, and thermal expansion

likelihood_criteria.low: Highland area (>1500m elevation), mean max temperature <28C
likelihood_criteria.medium: Lowland area, mean max temperature 28-33C, occasional heat waves
likelihood_criteria.high: Arid/semi-arid lowland, mean max temperature >33C, frequent extreme heat events (>38C)

consequence_criteria.minor: Gravel road (no asphalt to degrade), low traffic
consequence_criteria.moderate: Standard asphalt pavement, moderate traffic, some heavy vehicles
consequence_criteria.major: Thin asphalt overlay, heavy traffic, many heavy vehicles, standard bitumen grade

adaptation_measures: Use polymer-modified bitumen (PMB) for surface course (Skill #24)
Increase pavement thickness for thermal load distribution (Skill #21-23)
Use lighter-coloured aggregates to reduce heat absorption
Specify higher-grade bitumen (e.g., PG 76-22 for high-temperature zones)
Increase joint spacing in concrete pavements

hazards.description: Slope instability from rainfall saturation causing road blockage, formation loss, and debris flow

likelihood_criteria.low: Flat terrain (<5% gradient), stable geology, low rainfall
likelihood_criteria.medium: Moderate terrain (5-15% gradient), some weathered soils, moderate rainfall
likelihood_criteria.high: Steep terrain (>15% gradient), deeply weathered soils, high rainfall (>1500mm/yr), history of landslides

consequence_criteria.minor: Minor slope slip, road can be cleared quickly, alternative access exists
consequence_criteria.moderate: Significant road blockage, clearance takes days, limited alternatives
consequence_criteria.major: Major slope failure, road destroyed, no alternative, risk to life (settlements below road)

adaptation_measures: Improve slope drainage (herringbone drains, interceptor drains above road)
Reduce cut slope angles or provide benching
Install retaining structures at critical locations
Bioengineering (vetiver grass, deep-rooting vegetation) on slopes
Consider route realignment to avoid most vulnerable slopes
Install monitoring on critical slopes (inclinometers)

hazards.description: Extended dry periods causing subgrade desiccation, cracking (expansive soils), and loss of support

likelihood_criteria.low: High rainfall area (>1500mm/yr), short dry season (<2 months), non-expansive soils
likelihood_criteria.medium: Moderate rainfall, dry season 3-5 months, some expansive soils in subgrade
likelihood_criteria.high: Semi-arid area, dry season >5 months, expansive clay subgrade, increasing drought frequency

consequence_criteria.minor: Non-expansive subgrade, well-sealed pavement, limited moisture ingress
consequence_criteria.moderate: Some expansive soils, standard sealed surface, minor cracking observed
consequence_criteria.major: Expansive clay subgrade, thin pavement, unpaved shoulders allowing moisture ingress

adaptation_measures: Protect subgrade from desiccation using moisture barriers
Lime/cement stabilization for expansive clay subgrades (Skill #28)
Provide wider sealed shoulders to prevent moisture ingress
Specify flexible pavement structure accommodating minor movement
Avoid expansive clay as fill material in embankments

risk_matrix.description: Combined risk rating from likelihood and consequence

notes: Risk ratings are qualitative and require engineering judgment for project-specific application.
Multiple hazards may apply simultaneously -- assess each independently then consider interactions.
Climate adjustment factors in country modules provide quantitative values for design parameter adjustments.
Adaptation measures reference specific design skills for implementation details.
